posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You are worth more than what you're charging.

You know it. Your clients know it. And yet — the price you quote is still the one designed to make them say yes, not the one that reflects what you actually deliver.

Here's what low-ticket pricing actually costs you:

To earn $20,000/month at $1,000 per client, you need 20 clients.
20 onboarding calls. 20 sets of revisions. 20 invoice chases. 20 relationships to manage while trying to do great work.

At $5,000 per client — you need four.

Same revenue. Same month. Same you.
Four clients versus twenty.

The gap between those two realities is not your skills. It is not your experience. It is not your niche. It is your pricing architecture — and most service providers have never been taught how to build one.

The Premium Pricing Masterclass is the restructuring guide for service providers who are already delivering real results and are ready to be paid for them.

$9.99. The P.R.E.S.T.I.G.E™ framework. 18 pages.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/premium-pricing-masterclass

posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
Why do equally skilled service providers charge wildly different prices — and both get clients?

Because pricing is not about skill. It is about structure.

Here's the P.R.E.S.T.I.G.E™ framework from the Premium Pricing Masterclass:

P — Positioning: Shift from service provider to outcome architect. Not "I do social media" but "I build revenue-generating content systems for SaaS founders."

R — Results Definition: Define one specific, measurable, time-bound outcome. "Generate 30 qualified leads in 60 days" beats "improve your marketing" in every high-ticket sales conversation.

E — Elevation of Offer: Replace hourly rates with a 3-tier value architecture. Foundation ($3K–$5K), Authority ($7K–$12K), Elite ($15K+). Tiers create anchoring — 70% of buyers select the middle tier.

S — Scarcity Structuring: Premium pricing requires capacity control. "Maximum 5 clients per quarter" signals quality management, filters low-intent buyers, and increases perceived value.

T — Transformation Packaging: Package your offer as a journey from a painful current state to a specific destination. Name each phase. Make the transformation viscerally real before the client buys.

I — Investment Framing: Never justify price. Frame ROI. "At 15% improvement on your close rate, this investment returns itself in 45 days" is not a pitch — it's arithmetic.

G — Gravitas Sales Psychology: Spend 70% of every sales call diagnosing. The expert who asks better questions commands automatic authority.

E — Expansion & Scaling: Once one premium offer closes, systematize delivery, introduce referrals, build productized services.

Each layer multiplies the one before it. All eight running simultaneously = 3–5× price increase with maintained close rates.

👉 Full framework: https://publixion.com/guides/premium-pricing-masterclass

#Pricing #HighTicketSales #Consulting #Freelancing #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
The Premium Pricing Masterclass was built for a very specific person.

You'll know it's for you if:

→ You're a freelancer, consultant, coach, or agency owner currently charging $500–$2,000 per client — and you know your results are worth more

→ You write custom proposals for every single inquiry because you have no standardized offer, and every conversation starts from scratch

→ You've thought about raising your prices but the fear of losing clients has kept your rates frozen for 12+ months

→ You get on sales calls and leave them feeling like you were auditioning for the job rather than evaluating whether you want to take it

→ You deliver excellent results — clients stay, refer, and thank you — but you're not charging in proportion to the value you create

→ You've tried raising prices once, got one objection, and immediately dropped back to your old rate

This guide is not a mindset guide. It is not about "believing in your worth."

It is a structural blueprint — offer architecture, sales psychology, proposal framework, objection scripts, and a 30-day implementation calendar — for the service provider who is ready to be paid what their results are actually worth.

posts.linkedin:
Here's what a 30-day pricing restructure looks like in practice.

Meet Daniel. Marketing consultant. 5 years in. Charging $1,500 per client. Averaging 4 clients per month = $6,000/month. Working 55 hours per week.

He applied the Premium Pricing Masterclass over 30 days. Here's exactly what he did:

WEEK 1 — Positioning & Transformation

Day 3: Rewrote his one-sentence offer. Changed from "I manage marketing for small businesses" to "I help SaaS companies under $2M ARR generate 20+ qualified demos per month using a content-driven acquisition system."

Day 5: Updated LinkedIn headline, bio, and featured section with outcome language.

Day 7: Completed the Pricing Confidence Self-Assessment. Scored 38/80. Identified his two lowest dimensions: Investment Framing (3/10) and Authority Posture on calls (4/10).

WEEK 2 — Offer Packaging

Day 9: Built his 3-tier offer. Foundation ($3,500), Authority ($8,000), Elite ($14,000). Named each tier. Defined deliverables for each.

Day 12: Built his premium proposal template using the 7-section framework.

Day 14: Practiced all 5 objection scripts out loud. Recorded himself. Fixed the 2 that sounded defensive.

WEEK 3 — Sales Call Mastery

Day 17: Ran his first diagnostic call using the 5-stage framework. Prospect said "how do we move forward?" before he finished the presentation. Closed at $8,000.

WEEK 4 — Close & Optimize

Day 24: Second premium close. $8,000 Authority tier.
Day 28: Third prospect chose the Elite tier. $14,000.

Month 1 result: 3 clients. $30,000. Down from 4 clients at $6,000.

Same expertise. Different architecture.

👉 Get the Masterclass: https://publixion.com/guides/premium-pricing-masterclass

posts._note: Handle the top doubts: price, time, skill level, whether it will work for them.
posts.linkedin:
Every time I talk about raising prices, I get four objections. Let me address them honestly.

❌ "My clients won't pay that much."
✅ Your current clients won't — because you've trained them to expect low prices. New clients, positioned correctly with the right offer language and ROI framing, make buying decisions differently. The guide shows you how to attract the client who compares your fee to the value of the result, not to the cost of the task.

❌ "I don't have enough proof or case studies to justify higher prices."
✅ You have more proof than you think — you've just never documented it. The guide includes a framework for building your first case study from any successful engagement, regardless of whether you have formal testimonials. Your results exist. The system surfaces them.

❌ "I'll lose my current clients if I raise my rates."
✅ Some will leave. The ones who leave were never your premium clients — they were budget buyers who happened to find you before you found your real market. The guide includes a Pricing Increase Script specifically for raising rates with existing clients after delivering documented results.

❌ "I'm not confident enough to charge premium rates yet."
✅ Confidence follows structure, not the other way around. The guide gives you a tested 5-stage sales call framework, word-for-word closing scripts, and 5 objection responses. You don't need confidence to use a script. You need a script until confidence arrives.

Premium Pricing Masterclass — $9.99.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/premium-pricing-masterclass

#Pricing #Freelancing #Consulting #HighTicket #Publixion

The 4 objections people have before buying the Premium Pricing Masterclass — answered.

🚫 "My market won't pay premium prices."
✅ Every market has premium buyers. The guide teaches you how to position yourself so they find you — instead of the budget buyers who currently dominate your pipeline.

🚫 "I need more experience before I can charge more."
✅ Experience is not the variable. Positioning is. The guide shows you how to price based on the outcome you deliver — not the years on your CV.

🚫 "I already tried raising my prices and it didn't work."
✅ Raising a price without changing the offer structure is just charging more for the same thing. The P.R.E.S.T.I.G.E™ framework changes the architecture first — so the higher price feels inevitable, not arbitrary.

🚫 "$9.99 seems cheap for a pricing guide. Is it actually good?"
